reasoning: The root cause is identified as a failure or degradation in the F1 User Plane, specifically manifesting as high packet loss or increased latency. High packet loss or increased latency on the F1-U interface leads to performance degradation that is then detected and reported by the E2 interface (Control/User plane) and subsequently reflected in the A1/O1 interfaces (Non-RT RIC/Management plane).
actionable_steps: 1. Verify Physical Layer/Transport: Check the transport network between the O-CU and O-DU for packet loss, jitter, or link instability. 2. Check CU/DU Resource Utilization: Inspect the O-CU and O-DU for CPU/Memory spikes or buffer overflows that may be causing packet drops on the F1-U interface. 3. Restart F1-U User Plane Functions: If the hardware is healthy, restart the user-plane processing functions to clear any software-level hangs or deadlocks. 4. Validate Configuration: Ensure that the F1-U SCTP/GTP tunnels are correctly configured and not experiencing synchronization issues.
